summary: Matched four functions by small source-shape, signature, and call-routing changes: `dropcallback`, `un_80312904`, `gm_80173EEC`, and `gm_80164504`. The PR reports no behavior change, 100% objdiff for the four target functions, a clean `ninja diff`, and no functions broken by `diff_changes.py`.

key_files.role: Matched `dropcallback` by collapsing an explicit cached counter increment into direct post-increment array indexing.
Matched `un_80312904` by calling wrapper `un_803082F8(row->idx)` instead of `un_80308354(row->idx)`.
Adjusted `un_803082F8` and `un_80308354` return types and preserved another caller's shape with an explicit `(s16)` cast.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: adjusts `un_803082F8`/`un_80308354` return types with an `(s16)` cast

key_files.role: Updated declarations for recovered `s32` return types of `un_803082F8` and `un_80308354`.
Matched `gm_80173EEC` by routing repeated `gm_80160474` calls through a file-local inline wrapper.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: route `gm_80160474` calls through a file-local inline so result register allocation matches
diff:gm_16F1.c: adds `static inline bool gm_80173EEC_inline(u8 arg0, int arg1)`

key_files.role: Matched `gm_80164504` by changing return type from `int` to `void` and reordering two locals.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: return `void` and reorder two locals

what_changed: `dropcallback` changed from manually caching `HSD_Synth_804D7720`, incrementing it, and storing by the cached index to a single post-increment indexed store.
`un_80312904` now passes `un_803082F8(row->idx)` to `HSD_SisLib_803A6368` instead of calling `un_80308354(row->idx)` directly.
`un_803082F8` was changed from `void` to `s32` and now returns the result of `un_803063D4((s16) un_80308354(idx), 2, 0x128)`.
`un_80308354` was changed from returning `s16` to returning `s32`; `toy.h` was updated accordingly.
`gm_80173EEC` gained a file-local inline wrapper, `gm_80173EEC_inline`, around `gm_80160474`, and repeated call sites now use the wrapper.
`gm_80164504` changed from `int` to `void`; the header declaration was updated and local declaration order changed.
smart_moves: Updated owning headers alongside recovered return-type changes instead of leaving mismatched prototypes.
Kept the return-type/codegen adjustment narrow: `un_803082F8` uses an explicit `(s16)` cast where the PR says another caller needed that shape to keep matching.
Used a file-local inline wrapper only around the repeated `gm_80160474` calls needed for `gm_80173EEC` register allocation, rather than broadly changing the callee.
Verified both target-function matches and no collateral breakage according to the PR body and bot report.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered body proves that a function returns no value or returns a wider value than previously declared, update both the implementation and owning header so call sites and prototypes remain truthful.
evidence_refs: diff:gm_1601.c and gm_1601.h change `gm_80164504` from `int` to `void`
diff:toy.c and toy.h change `un_803082F8`/`un_80308354` return types to `s32`

decomp_lessons.lesson: A wrapper call can be the authored or matching source shape for a caller even when the wrapped callee appears equivalent; here `un_80312904` matched by calling `un_803082F8` rather than `un_80308354` directly.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: `un_80312904`: call the `un_803082F8` wrapper instead of `un_80308354`

decomp_lessons.lesson: Expression shape can affect MWCC matching: a direct post-increment array index may match where an equivalent cached-index sequence does not.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: collapse cached counter into direct post-increment

naming_conventions.convention: The new helper was named after its containing function as `gm_80173EEC_inline`, reflecting a narrow file-local codegen helper rather than a semantic API rename.
evidence_refs: diff:gm_16F1.c: `static inline bool gm_80173EEC_inline(u8 arg0, int arg1)`

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use direct post-increment indexing to influence MWCC scheduling/register allocation for a counter-backed array append.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Equivalent C expressions should still be checked against authored-source style and objdiff; this PR supports the tactic only for this narrow case.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Route repeated calls through a file-local inline wrapper to affect the result register allocation at call sites.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: file-local inline so result register allocation matches
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Inline helper boundaries are codegen-sensitive; retain only with objdiff evidence and avoid treating file-local wrappers as a blanket cleanup.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Change local declaration order when needed for MWCC register allocation or stack/local shape.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Declaration-order changes are a matching tactic and need verification evidence; this PR reports objdiff and clean diff verification.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use explicit narrowing casts at wrapper boundaries to preserve caller matching after return-type changes.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: `(s16)` cast to keep the other caller matching
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Do not generalize the cast beyond the specific caller/return-type evidence.

follow_up_queries: un_803082F8 un_80308354 return type s32 s16 cast
gm_80164504 void return type local reorder
MWCC declaration order register allocation matching

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR number/title/state/author/timestamps and changed-file list
human_pr_text: matching percentages, no behavior change statement, tactics summary, verification commands
raw_diff: exact edits across seven files
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot reports four new 100% matches
notes: No review comments were present in the slice, so reviewer intent or requested changes cannot be inferred.
The PR body says the summary is AI-assisted; intake treats it as PR evidence for what changed, not as an independent accepted standard.
Verification claims are from the PR body and bot report only; no new build, objdiff, or lint was run during intake.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Direct post-increment array append can affect matching versus cached-index increment
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2764, `dropcallback` reached 100% by replacing a cached counter/increment/store sequence with `HSD_Synth_804C28E0[HSD_Synth_804D7720++] = v`; the PR reports objdiff confirmation and clean `ninja diff`.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Narrow file-local inline wrapper used to steer result register allocation
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2764, `gm_80173EEC` reached 100% by adding `static inline bool gm_80173EEC_inline(u8 arg0, int arg1)` around `gm_80160474` and routing repeated call sites through it; the PR says this made the result register allocation match.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Header prototypes updated with recovered return types
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2764 changed `gm_80164504` from `int` to `void` in both source and header, and changed `un_803082F8`/`un_80308354` to `s32` in both `toy.c` and `toy.h`, matching the standard that proven signatures should update the owning header.

source_update_candidates.title: Document narrow file-local inline wrappers as a codegen tactic requiring verifier evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2764 provides an example where a file-local inline wrapper around an existing function changed MWCC result register allocation enough to match `gm_80173EEC`. If curated, this should be framed as a narrow tactic requiring objdiff/regression evidence, not as general cleanup.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: `gm_80173EEC`: route calls through a file-local inline so result register allocation matches
source_update_candidates.reason: The existing standards cover matching tactics needing evidence; this PR may be a useful concrete example for curator-owned standards or examples.

source_update_candidates.title: Document wrapper-call selection as a possible authored/callsite matching shape
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2764 matched `un_80312904` by calling `un_803082F8` rather than directly calling `un_80308354`, alongside return-type adjustments in `toy.c`/`toy.h`. If curated, this can become an example that wrapper boundaries may matter for callsite matching and should be tested before flattening equivalent calls.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: `un_80312904`: call the `un_803082F8` wrapper instead of `un_80308354`
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful as a standards example if supported by additional PRs or local source evidence; this PR alone supports only a narrow case.

rejection_notes: Do not promote `gm_80173EEC_inline` as a general naming convention for all inline helpers; evidence only shows this one file-local helper.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale beyond merge state; the slice contains no review comments.
Do not generalize local declaration reordering as preferred style; the PR presents it as a matching/codegen tactic with verification.
